red_flags: radiating to left arm or jaw
action: Call 911 immediately. Do not drive yourself to the hospital. Chew 325mg aspirin if not allergic and no contraindications. Loosen tight clothing. Stay calm and still.

red_flags: use of neck muscles to breathe
action: Call 911 immediately. Sit upright, leaning slightly forward. Use prescribed inhaler if available. Do not leave the patient alone.
Call 911 immediately. Check for pulse and breathing. Begin CPR if trained and no pulse is present. Do not give anything by mouth.
Call 911 immediately. This could indicate a brain bleed. Lie still in a quiet dark room. Do not take NSAIDs.
Call 911 immediately. Note the time symptoms started. Do not give food or water. FAST: Face drooping, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call 911.
Call 911 immediately. Use epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) if available. Lie flat with legs raised unless breathing is difficult. Do not stand up.

red_flags: blood does not stop after 10 minutes of pressure
action: Call 911 immediately. Apply firm direct pressure with clean cloth. Do not remove the cloth if saturated, add more on top. Elevate the injured limb if possible.
Call 911 for first seizure or prolonged seizure. Protect from injury, turn on side, do not restrain. Never put anything in the mouth. Time the seizure.

symptom: high fever in infant under 3 months
action: Seek emergency care immediately for any fever in infant under 3 months. Do not wait. This age group can deteriorate rapidly.
source: NIH MedlinePlus, WHO child triage guidelines

action: Call 911 immediately and Poison Control (1-800-222-1222 in US). Do not induce vomiting unless instructed. Bring the substance container to the hospital.

symptom: severe headache with fever and neck stiffness
red_flags: triad of fever neck stiffness altered consciousness
action: Call 911 immediately. Bacterial meningitis is life-threatening and time-critical. Do not wait for all symptoms to appear.

Contact 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline (call or text 988 in US) or go to nearest emergency department immediately. Remove access to means. Do not leave the person alone. Stay and listen without judgment.

red_flags: confusion and agitation after stopping alcohol
action: Seek emergency care. Alcohol withdrawal can be fatal. Do not stop alcohol abruptly without medical supervision in heavy dependent drinkers. Benzodiazepines are the first-line treatment in a clinical setting.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM F10.239, NIH MedlinePlus, NCBI StatPearls

red_flags: elderly or homeless patient found in cold
action: Call 911. Move to warm environment. Remove wet clothing. Apply warm blankets. Do not rub the skin vigorously. Handle gently as hypothermic heart is prone to arrhythmia.

red_flags: absence of sweating with hot skin
action: Call 911 immediately. Move to cool environment. Apply cool water or ice packs to neck, armpits, and groin. Do not give fluids by mouth to unconscious patient.
Seek emergency care or call 911. Sit upright. Use prescribed inhalers. Do not lie flat. Monitor oxygen saturation if pulse oximeter available.

red_flags: right lower quadrant pain with fever
action: Seek emergency care. Do not eat or drink. Do not take painkillers before evaluation as they may mask symptoms. Lie in a comfortable position.
Seek urgent medical evaluation. Take prescribed fever reducers. Stay hydrated. Seek emergency care if altered mental status or temperature above 40C/104F.
Seek emergency care. Check blood glucose if diabetic. Do not leave alone. Note time of onset and any precipitating events.
Seek urgent medical evaluation. Rest, avoid exertion. Do not suppress the cough entirely. Seek emergency care if blood volume is large.
Seek emergency care immediately. Do not eat or drink. Lie down and remain calm. Note volume and color of blood.
Seek urgent medical evaluation. Avoid NSAIDs and blood thinners. Maintain hydration. Seek emergency care if accompanied by dizziness or fainting.
Seek emergency evaluation immediately. Retinal artery occlusion has a narrow treatment window. Cover but do not press on the affected eye.

red_flags: pain that starts around navel and moves to lower right
action: Seek emergency evaluation immediately. Do not eat, drink, or take laxatives. This presentation requires urgent surgical assessment to rule out appendicitis.
Seek emergency care for non-blanching rash with fever. Press a glass against the rash to check if it blanches. Non-blanching rash with fever requires immediate evaluation.

red_flags: severe eye pain with nausea and vomiting
action: Seek urgent ophthalmology evaluation. Acute glaucoma is a medical emergency. Do not press on the eye. Avoid bright lights.
Seek emergency care immediately if pregnant or possibly pregnant. Ectopic pregnancy can be life-threatening. Seek urgent care for sudden severe pelvic pain in any woman.
Seek urgent medical evaluation. Septic arthritis requires prompt diagnosis and treatment to prevent permanent joint damage. Do not delay evaluation.

red_flags: nausea and vomiting with testicular pain
action: Seek emergency evaluation immediately for sudden severe testicular pain. Testicular torsion is a surgical emergency with a 6-hour window for testicular salvage. Do not delay.
Seek urgent medical evaluation within 24 to 48 hours. Avoid alcohol completely. Confusion with jaundice may indicate liver failure and requires emergency care.

red_flags: associated shortness of breath or sweating
action: Stop activity immediately and rest. Seek urgent cardiac evaluation. Use prescribed nitrates if available. Seek emergency care if pain does not resolve with rest within 15 minutes.
Seek urgent medical evaluation. Delirium in elderly is often caused by a treatable underlying condition. Do not attribute to dementia without assessment. Ensure patient safety.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM R41.0, NIH MedlinePlus, NCBI StatPearls

action: If conscious and able to swallow: give 15g fast-acting carbohydrate (glucose tablets, juice, regular soda). Recheck glucose in 15 minutes. Call 911 if unconscious. Do not give anything by mouth to unconscious patient.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM E16.0, NIH MedlinePlus, ADA guidelines

red_flags: no relief after 2 to 3 puffs of rescue inhaler
peak flow below 50% of personal best
action: Use rescue bronchodilator inhaler immediately. Sit upright, leaning slightly forward. Call 911 if no improvement after 2 doses. Do not lie down. Severe attacks require emergency treatment.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM J45.901, NIH MedlinePlus, GINA guidelines

red_flags: change in sputum color and volume
action: Use prescribed rescue bronchodilators and start oral steroids if prescribed. Seek urgent medical evaluation. Call 911 if severe or rapidly worsening. Controlled oxygen therapy is important.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM J44.1, NIH MedlinePlus, GOLD guidelines

action: Seek urgent medical evaluation. Antibiotics are required. Seek emergency care if confusion, low blood pressure, or low oxygen saturation present. High-risk patients may need hospital admission.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM J18.9, NIH MedlinePlus, NICE guidelines

red_flags: unilateral leg swelling with calf pain
recent surgery immobility or long flight
action: Seek urgent medical evaluation. DVT requires anticoagulation therapy. DVT can progress to pulmonary embolism. Seek emergency care if accompanied by shortness of breath or chest pain.

red_flags: weight gain of more than 2kg in 2 days
action: Seek urgent medical evaluation. Sit upright. Avoid salt and excess fluids. Seek emergency care for acute pulmonary edema. Daily weight monitoring is important for known heart failure patients.

red_flags: only kidney or renal transplant patient
action: Seek urgent medical evaluation. Take OTC pain relievers and stay hydrated. Fever with kidney stone pain is a urological emergency indicating infected obstruction. Most small stones pass spontaneously.

red_flags: food restriction with distorted body image
action: Seek specialist eating disorder evaluation. Contact National Alliance for Eating Disorders helpline at 1-866-662-1235. Medical complications of eating disorders can be life-threatening. GP assessment for physical complications is needed urgently.

red_flags: pain that reliably starts after fixed distance and stops with rest
sudden severe pain with pale pulseless leg
action: Seek medical evaluation. Sudden cold pulseless leg is a vascular emergency requiring immediate care. Claudication warrants cardiovascular risk assessment.
Rest and stay hydrated. Take paracetamol or ibuprofen as directed. Monitor temperature every 4 hours. Seek medical attention if fever exceeds 39.5C or lasts more than 72 hours.
Sit or lie down immediately to prevent falls. Rise slowly from sitting or lying positions. Seek care if persistent, sudden, or accompanied by neurological symptoms.
Seek medical evaluation. Avoid caffeine, alcohol, and stimulants. Seek emergency care if palpitations are accompanied by chest pain, fainting, or severe shortness of breath.

red_flags: sudden unilateral swelling with calf pain
recent immobility or surgery or flight
action: Elevate legs when resting. Seek urgent evaluation for sudden unilateral swelling, which may indicate DVT. Seek emergency care if accompanied by shortness of breath.

red_flags: painless blood in urine over age 40
action: Seek medical evaluation within 24 to 48 hours. Increase fluid intake. Painless hematuria in adults over 40 requires urgent investigation to rule out malignancy.

red_flags: loss of more than 5% body weight in 6-12 months
action: Schedule GP appointment for full assessment including blood tests. Maintain nutritional intake. This symptom requires investigation to identify underlying cause.
Schedule GP appointment. Night sweats with weight loss and fever are a concerning triad requiring investigation. Keep a diary of frequency and severity.

red_flags: associated weight loss or night sweats
action: Schedule GP appointment. Hard fixed lymph nodes require urgent evaluation. Tender lymph nodes following recent infection are usually reactive and less concerning.

red_flags: associated with frequent urination and weight loss
action: Seek medical evaluation. Excessive thirst with frequent urination and weight loss is the classic triad of diabetes and requires prompt blood glucose testing.
Seek urgent medical evaluation. Ascending weakness or weakness with breathing difficulty may indicate Guillain-Barre syndrome, which is a neurological emergency.

red_flags: progressive difficulty swallowing solids then liquids
action: Seek medical evaluation within days. Seek emergency care if swallowing difficulty is sudden, associated with breathing difficulty, or caused by a lodged object.

red_flags: memory loss with confusion or agitation
action: Schedule GP appointment for cognitive assessment. Sudden onset memory loss is an emergency. Ensure patient safety at home.
Seek GP appointment. If experiencing thoughts of suicide or self-harm, contact a crisis line (988 in US) or go to nearest emergency department. Maintain social connections and regular routine.
Seek medical evaluation within 2 weeks. Any new breast lump requires clinical and radiological assessment. Do not wait and see. Perform regular self-examination.
Seek medical evaluation. Postmenopausal bleeding requires urgent evaluation to exclude endometrial cancer. Intermenstrual bleeding should always be investigated.

red_flags: soaking pad or tampon every hour for several consecutive hours
new onset heavy bleeding after age 40
action: Seek medical evaluation. Track cycle length and flow. Seek emergency care if bleeding is excessively heavy. Iron supplementation may be needed for associated anemia.

red_flags: associated headache visual changes or chest pain
action: Seek emergency care for blood pressure above 180/120 mmHg with symptoms. Hypertensive urgency or emergency requires prompt treatment. Take prescribed medications. Monitor blood pressure regularly.

red_flags: no urine output for 8 or more hours
action: Drink oral rehydration solution in small frequent sips. Avoid alcohol and caffeine. Seek emergency care if unable to keep fluids down, confusion develops, or signs of severe dehydration appear.
Seek urgent ophthalmology evaluation within 24 hours for sudden onset floaters with flashes. Retinal detachment is a surgical emergency. Do not wait.

red_flags: syncope in older adult or heart disease patient
action: Lie flat with legs elevated if prodrome occurs. Seek medical evaluation. Exertional syncope or syncope with palpitations requires urgent cardiac evaluation.

red_flags: new onset chest pain or palpitations after COVID-19
action: Schedule GP appointment for post-COVID assessment. Rest and paced activity. Seek urgent care for new chest pain or breathlessness post-COVID. Document symptom progression.

red_flags: excessive thirst and urination with weight loss
action: Seek medical evaluation for blood glucose testing. Fruity breath with confusion and rapid breathing may indicate diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), which requires emergency care.

Isolate. Rest and stay hydrated. Monitor oxygen saturation if pulse oximeter available. Seek emergency care if red flags appear. High-risk individuals should contact GP for antiviral treatment options early.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM U07.1, NIH MedlinePlus, CDC COVID guidelines

red_flags: high risk groups elderly pregnant immunocompromised
action: Rest, stay hydrated, take antipyretics. Antiviral medications (oseltamivir) are most effective if started within 48 hours of symptom onset in high-risk patients. Seek urgent care if red flags develop.
source: CDC ICD-10-CM J11.1, NIH MedlinePlus, CDC influenza guidelines

red_flags: sudden hearing loss in one ear
action: Seek urgent medical evaluation for sudden hearing loss in one ear, which is a medical emergency with a narrow treatment window. Routine hearing loss should be evaluated by GP.
Schedule GP appointment and sleep study referral. Sleep apnea increases cardiovascular risk. CPAP therapy is effective. Weight loss reduces severity. Avoid alcohol and sedatives.

red_flags: severe pain out of proportion to rash
rash affecting ear with facial weakness
action: Seek medical evaluation for antiviral therapy within 72 hours of rash onset. Keep rash covered. Pain management is important. Seek urgent care for eye involvement (herpes zoster ophthalmicus).

red_flags: severe pain radiating to right shoulder
action: Seek medical evaluation. Avoid fatty foods. Fever with right upper quadrant pain and jaundice suggests cholangitis and requires emergency care.
Rest in a quiet dark room. Stay hydrated. Take OTC pain relievers as directed. Seek medical attention if headache is severe, persistent over 72 hours, or accompanied by red flags.

red_flags: cannot keep fluids down for 24 hours
action: Sip clear fluids slowly. Avoid solid foods until vomiting settles. Seek care if vomiting persists more than 24 hours, blood is present, or severe dehydration occurs.
Stay hydrated with oral rehydration solutions. Avoid dairy, fatty and spicy foods temporarily. Seek medical attention if diarrhea lasts more than 7 days, blood is present, or dehydration occurs.
Rest, stay hydrated, use honey or lozenges for symptom relief. Seek medical attention if cough persists more than 3 weeks, worsens, or is accompanied by red flags.
Gargle warm salt water. Use OTC throat lozenges and pain relievers. Seek medical evaluation for throat swab if fever is present. Seek emergency care if swallowing or breathing is affected.

red_flags: discharge lasting more than 10 days without improvement
action: Rest, stay hydrated, use saline nasal rinse. OTC antihistamines or decongestants may help allergic rhinitis. Seek care if symptoms persist more than 10 days or worsen significantly.

red_flags: unexplained fatigue lasting more than 6 weeks
action: Ensure adequate sleep, hydration, and nutrition. Reduce stress. Schedule GP appointment if fatigue persists more than 2 weeks without clear cause.

red_flags: single hot swollen joint with fever
morning stiffness lasting more than 1 hour
action: Rest the affected joint. Apply ice for acute pain, heat for chronic stiffness. Use OTC anti-inflammatories as directed. Seek urgent care for single hot swollen joint with fever (septic arthritis).

red_flags: pain radiating down leg with weakness
loss of bladder or bowel control
action: Rest with gentle activity. Apply heat or ice. Use OTC pain relievers. Seek urgent care if red flags are present, particularly bladder or bowel changes which may indicate cauda equina syndrome.
Increase fluid intake. Seek medical evaluation for antibiotic prescription. Avoid caffeine and alcohol. Seek urgent care if fever or flank pain suggests kidney involvement.

Identify and avoid the trigger. Use mild soap and fragrance-free moisturizers. OTC hydrocortisone cream for mild contact dermatitis. Seek medical evaluation if rash is spreading, blistering, or not improving.

red_flags: chest pain not relieved by antacids
action: Eat smaller meals, avoid lying down after eating, elevate head of bed. Use OTC antacids or H2 blockers. Seek medical evaluation if symptoms are frequent, severe, or accompanied by red flags.
Increase fluid and fibre intake. Gentle physical activity. OTC laxatives for short-term use. Seek medical evaluation if no bowel movement for more than 5 days or if red flags are present.

red_flags: no passage of gas or stool
pain with bloating in postmenopausal women
action: Avoid gas-producing foods. Increase fibre and water intake. Schedule GP appointment if persistent or recurrent. Seek urgent care if accompanied by inability to pass gas or stool.

red_flags: first episode indistinguishable from cardiac event
associated chest pain and shortness of breath in older adults
action: Practice slow controlled breathing. Seek GP assessment to exclude medical causes. First episode panic attack warrants medical evaluation to exclude cardiac or thyroid causes.

red_flags: insomnia with daytime sleepiness and snoring
sudden onset insomnia with fever or confusion
action: Practice sleep hygiene: regular schedule, dark cool room, no screens before bed. Seek GP evaluation if persistent over 4 weeks. Cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) is first-line treatment.

red_flags: sudden onset affecting one side of body
action: Seek medical evaluation. Sudden onset unilateral numbness is a stroke warning sign and requires emergency assessment. Gradual bilateral symptoms warrant scheduled GP evaluation.

red_flags: ear pain with fever and discharge
action: Use OTC pain relievers. Seek medical evaluation if fever is present, discharge occurs, or hearing is affected. Avoid inserting anything into the ear canal.
Avoid rubbing the eye. Use lubricating eye drops. Seek medical evaluation if there is pain, vision change, or photophobia. Seek emergency care if chemical exposure or acute glaucoma is suspected.

red_flags: nighttime urination waking from sleep repeatedly
action: Monitor fluid intake. Seek medical evaluation. New onset frequent urination with excessive thirst warrants blood glucose testing to exclude diabetes.

red_flags: neck stiffness with fever and headache
pain with swallowing or voice change
action: Apply heat for muscle tension. Gentle stretching and movement. Seek emergency care if neck pain follows trauma or is accompanied by fever and stiff neck.
Rest, ice, compression, elevation (RICE) for acute injury. Use OTC anti-inflammatories. Seek urgent evaluation for trauma with swelling or inability to bear weight.
Avoid known triggers. Use moisturizers and mild soap. OTC antihistamines for allergic causes. Seek medical evaluation if generalized itching occurs without rash as it may indicate systemic disease.

red_flags: associated with weight loss or bowel symptoms
painless ulcer in smoker or alcohol user
action: Use topical analgesics and antiseptic mouthwashes. Avoid spicy and acidic foods. Any ulcer lasting more than 3 weeks must be evaluated by a clinician to exclude malignancy.

red_flags: bleeding not stopping after 20 minutes of pressure
action: Pinch soft part of nose and lean forward for 10 to 20 minutes. Breathe through the mouth. Seek medical care if bleeding does not stop after 20 minutes or if it is recurrent and heavy.

red_flags: hoarseness lasting more than 3 weeks in smoker or alcohol user
action: Rest the voice and stay hydrated. Seek urgent evaluation if hoarseness persists more than 3 weeks especially in smokers as laryngeal cancer must be excluded.

red_flags: blood mixed in stool rather than on surface
action: Seek medical evaluation. Do not assume hemorrhoids without clinical assessment. Colorectal cancer must be excluded especially in adults over 40 or those with red flag symptoms.

red_flags: pain with intercourse or bowel movements
action: NSAIDs taken 1 to 2 days before period onset can help. Heat application. Seek GP evaluation if pain is severe, worsening, or interfering with daily activities as endometriosis requires diagnosis.
Stay home and isolate. Use calamine lotion and antihistamines for itch. Avoid scratching. Antiviral therapy required for high-risk groups. Seek urgent care for immunocompromised patients.

red_flags: swelling of lips tongue or throat
action: Take OTC antihistamine for mild hives. Remove or avoid allergen. Seek emergency care immediately if any throat swelling, breathing difficulty, or dizziness develops.
Cool the skin with cool water. Apply aloe vera or moisturizing lotion. Stay hydrated. Avoid further sun exposure. Seek medical care for blistering over large area or fever.
Schedule GP appointment. Avoid loud noises and use ear protection. Pulsatile tinnitus or unilateral tinnitus requires more urgent evaluation to exclude vascular or tumour causes.

red_flags: associated with slowing of movement and rigidity
action: Schedule GP appointment. Note whether tremor occurs at rest or with movement. Avoid caffeine and alcohol. Alcohol withdrawal tremor may require urgent medical management.

red_flags: associated with weight changes or fatigue
hair loss in women of childbearing age
action: Schedule GP appointment. Blood tests for thyroid function, iron levels, and hormones are usually needed. Avoid tight hairstyles and harsh chemical treatments.

red_flags: sleepiness causing safety risk while driving
associated with snoring and witnessed apnea
action: Schedule GP appointment. Avoid driving if excessively drowsy. Sleep study (polysomnography) may be required. Weight loss can help obstructive sleep apnea.

red_flags: calf cramp with unilateral swelling and redness
action: Stretch the affected muscle gently. Ensure adequate hydration and electrolyte intake. Seek medical evaluation for recurrent calf cramps with swelling, which may indicate DVT.

red_flags: severe palpitations with weight loss and tremor
symptoms of thyroid storm: fever agitation rapid heart rate
action: Schedule GP appointment for thyroid function tests. Thyroid storm is a critical emergency. Any thyroid mass with hoarseness warrants urgent evaluation.

red_flags: new onset under age 40 without psychological stress
action: Schedule GP appointment. Erectile dysfunction in middle-aged men may be the first sign of cardiovascular disease. Cardiovascular risk assessment is recommended.

red_flags: finger color changes from white to blue to red with cold exposure
associated with joint pain and skin thickening
action: Keep hands and feet warm. Avoid smoking and caffeine. Seek medical evaluation if color changes occur regularly or if skin breaks down. May indicate connective tissue disorder.

red_flags: night sweats with weight loss and fever
sweating during hypoglycemic episodes in diabetics
action: Schedule GP appointment. Night sweats with weight loss and fever in any patient require investigation. Daytime sweating in controlled settings may indicate endocrine or autonomic disorder.

red_flags: first episode with chest pain or palpitations
associated with weight loss and tremor
action: Practice slow diaphragmatic breathing: in for 4, hold for 4, out for 4. Grounding techniques: focus on 5 things you can see. Seek GP evaluation after first episode to exclude medical causes.
Rest and splint for acute strain. Wrist brace for carpal tunnel syndrome. Seek evaluation for trauma with deformity or severe pain. Physiotherapy is often helpful.

red_flags: hip pain after fall in elderly
action: Seek emergency evaluation for elderly patient with hip pain after fall. Rest for acute pain. OTC anti-inflammatories. Hip fracture is a serious injury in the elderly requiring immediate care.

red_flags: left shoulder pain with chest tightness in older adults
inability to raise arm at all
action: Rest and ice for acute injury. Physical therapy and exercise for chronic conditions. Seek emergency care if left shoulder pain occurs with chest symptoms. GP evaluation for persistent pain.
